insight: Thunderhead can be used as an early rush unit to deny moderate early-game pressure, not just as late-game soak. Getting it too early causes the opponent to simply not build attack; getting it too late means they kill it to deny absorb, so timing is critical.
author: amalloy
context: A newer player asked about rushing Thunderhead early and amalloy confirmed both early and late uses are valid while explaining the timing risks.

insight: When a big absorber is present, the primary strategic priority is building the most efficient permanent attack rather than conditional or burst attackers like Grenade Mech.
author: ruinedshadows
context: A newer player described losing after building Grenade Mechs and Tarsiers; ruinedshadows and medar explained efficient permanent attack is preferred against large absorbers.

insight: Units with special upsides (e.g., Grenade Mech burst damage) are generally weak unless you can specifically exploit that upside; units that are harder to set up or awkward to use are often stronger but require evaluating whether the setup cost is worth it.
author: medar
context: Discussion helping a newer player understand how to evaluate conditional and special-upside attackers in a random set.

insight: A practical method for evaluating attackers in a given set is comparing cost-to-attack ratios to identify the most efficient permanent attack; this heuristic is often sufficient to win without needing deep knowledge of every unit's special ability.
author: ruinedshadows
context: ruinedshadows advised a newer player on how to evaluate which attacker to build when the set is unfamiliar.
